chapter_title.en: Chapter on What Allah {swt}, the Almighty, Has Made Permissible and Prohibited in Marriage

text:
4475 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ قَيْسٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ :
"أَنَّهُ قَضَى فِي رَجُلٍ تَزَوَّجَ اِمْرَأَةً وَ أَصْدَقَتْهُ هِيَ وَ اِشْتَرَطَتْ عَلَيْهِ "أَنَّ بِيَدِهَا اَلْجِمَاعَ وَ اَلطَّلاَقَ"
قَالَ "خَالَفَتِ اَلسُّنَّةَ وَ وُلِّيَتْ حَقّاً لَيْسَتْ بِأَهْلِهِ " فَقَضَى "أَنَّ عَلَيْهِ اَلصَّدَاقَ وَ بِيَدِهِ اَلْجِمَاعَ وَ اَلطَّلاَقَ وَ ذَلِكَ اَلسُّنَّةُ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.4475 - Muhammad ibn Qays narrated from Abu Ja'far Imam Al-Baqir (as):
Imam (as) ruled regarding a man who married a woman, and she gave him the dowry herself and stipulated that she would have the authority over intimacy and divorce.
Imam (as) said: "She has opposed the Sunnah and has been given a right she is not entitled to." Then Imam (as) ruled: "The dowry is obligatory upon him, and the consummation and divorce are in his hands, as this is the Sunnah."
